rawContent: Task: 260409-220738 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 260409-220738 (verify existing health check endpoint) completed all 7 stages with 0 retries, but verify stage reported FAIL due to test assertion errors in date formatting and lint errors in src/pages/auth/register.tsx — code completely outside the task scope (which was only src/app/api/health/route.ts and route.test.ts). Despite verify FAIL, pipeline proceeded through review-fix to ship and completed, suggesting code that failed validation was shipped. This is the fifteenth consecutive run exhibiting the verify-to-ship gate flaw. | Pattern: This is the fifteenth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, two 419-reruns, 847, 846, 858, 863, 874, 260409-215133, and now 260409-220738) exhibiting the identical verify-to-ship gate flaw where verify stage FAIL does not prevent pipeline progression to ship. The suggestion to implement a verifyExitCode gate has been recommended fourteen times without implementation. Notably this run's verify errors were in register.tsx which is outside the task scope, suggesting verify is running full-suite linting rather than scoped validation. | Suggestion: Implement the verifyExitCode conditional gate in the ship stage template. This is the fifteenth consecutive run with the same flaw — enough evidence that this is a structural defect, not an edge case. Add a check that reads the verify stage exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed validation. The verify stage should only report FAIL for files within the task scope, and ship should not accept FAIL status regardless of mode. | Flaw: verify → ship gate — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage. Verify reported FAIL (date formatting test failures, lint errors in register.tsx) yet ship stage completed with 0 retries and shipped code. | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
